channel.title: Comments for page "Antimemetics Division Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Antimemetics Division Hub"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, one of the best series on the site. I really need to catch up on th last year of updates.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why isn’t this a canon yet? It has the requisite five works and three authors.

aaaaaaAAAAAAAAAÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄÄ
It's Tuesday March 10 2020, 1:19AM. There's no story updates.its dead bois.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this is probably my favourite foundation canon, even if every damn story breaks my heart. I'm dying for the next chapter. I check the hub page every few days looking for it. really fantastic world building here.

item.title: Phenomenal!
item.content:encoded:
This whole thing is why I finally decided to make an account.. it's just too good to not start throwing votes around.

The entire idea of antimemetics really plays on some of my biggest fears and insecurities, and I'd imagine those of most of humanity in general. Knowledge is power, after all, but ignorance is also bliss. This kind of crap is why I'm up at 4AM.

Great work, qntm (by the way, is it pronounced 'quantum'?) and please do keep it up. I'd also like to thank sirpudding for bringing me here - your stuff's next, buddy.

+1 only because I can't give any more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can, of course
(Thank you <3)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yoo that looks really damn cool!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's rad! Can I share it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love the antimemetic division, so I've made a fanart <3

https://cdn.discordapp.com/attachments/344973115303919619/572697643248254986/20190430_101453.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's not a coincidence. I was very inspired specifically by SCP-1425 and the Star Signals cult. That's why I have a chapter set in Ojai called "Ojai", that's why I trwoll flrr with the vlth dialogue in the same way, that's why I'm talking about mirrors and holes.

While writing CASE COLOURLESS GREEN I had a conversation with Silber about the background of SCP-1425, because I was thinking of using the Star Signals cult as a major antagonist for the Antimemetics Division. We threw various ideas around for developing that further. However, looking back at that conversation, it looks as if a lot of my intentions from that time have morphed somewhat between then and now!

As of right now, the connection between the Antimemetics Division continuity and the Fifthist continuity is intentionally rather tenuous and potentially contradictory. There are a few reasons for this. One is that I like having more creative autonomy and doing my own thing. Another is that I think linking things together kind of shrinks the universe rather than expanding it. I think inventing a common explanation for multiple SCPs, linking them together into a single continuity, limits what's possible in the reader's imagination.

In fact, for a very long time, my concept for SCP-3125 was that it was a monster which ate researchers who were too successful at coming up with unified theories for SCPs. If you discovered a common explanation, the common explanation itself would come alive and kill you! This would be an in-universe rationale for why the Foundation has no larger theories for where SCPs come from. This would be why there is no canon. It would be because canon is the monster. But I went in a different direction.

So anyway, stay tuned for the next chapter of Five Five Five Five Five. I'm very close to being so tired of working on it that I release it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is there any fifthist influence with 3125? Because it feels like there's a weird number of coincidences with the whole 5-5-5-5-5 thing if they're unrelated. It's also mentioned that 1425 is a possible aspect of 3125, and the starfish-like thing mentioned in CASE COLOURLESS GREEN seems like it would tie in well with weird fifthist bullshit their pantheon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I recognize the irony in this whole… thing… getting a relatively small amount of attention compared to other stuff like Marshall, Carter and Dark or the Chaos Insurgency, but I also feel like it deserves more.

item.content:encoded: I write this. What's your question?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey. Uh. Question. How do I contact the people who write this cause I have an idea? or theory? and would like to tell them.

item.content:encoded: I bet my one of my kidneys that you are secretly a famous writer in real life.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
… I just realized I posted this for some reason. long, long ago.

Anyhoo, this is pretty close to becoming a canon. Figure's as this is a pretty intriguing series. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this is my favorite hub on the SCP site. i don't really have anything useful to offer here like a question or criticism I just really like this hub and thought you should know

item.title: Antimemetic bomb
item.content:encoded: How exactly does it work? For an ordinary bomb to work, it needs to be close to it's target, but what is the equivalent of that for the antimemetic bomb from Unforgettable, that's what you are?

item.title: (no title)
(no title)
item.content:encoded:
Regarding 2. I assume poor containment is from poor understanding? Because as we understand nuclear matter more, scientists are able to prevent themselves from the fate of Madame Curie.

And I think such poor understanding is because their subject.

Regarding 3. Isn't a loss of personnel itself enough to trigger re-staffing process? Or outside of 3125 chamber, antimemetic division has always been this small?

I'd assume "Yes, we're missing people, reason is above your clearance" would be good enough for HR to give them new hires. Although not being able to articulate why even when an O5 ask might hurt heir case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. You're right that memetics and antimemetics are two sides of the same coin. It's more a question of specialism than anything. A single idea's memeticity is highly subjective, it can have memetic and antimemetic aspects depending on the host. SCP-3125 in particular is nominally a highly contagious meme (so, memetic) with powerful camouflaging ability which means nobody knows about it (so, antimemetic). Researchers from either side of the spectrum are equally likely to uncover its existence. It's a bit of a mess.

2. The same reason a nuclear research organisation would become highly radioactive during their research: poor containment.

3. Probably a combination of the two. But also: anybody who actually ran the numbers and worked out why they were declining would be immediately eaten by the cause of that decline! (Or, if they were smart, they would spot the alarming pattern early and deliberately stop looking into it for their own safety.) And of course, eventually the numbers fell so low that there was nobody left to look into it.

4. In my book, yes, SCP-2521 is an antimeme, but only in the same mundane, "mechanical" way that the content of the stolen files in a spy thriller is an antimeme. Holding the information is dangerous to you because someone will come after you; sharing the information with someone else is dangerous to them too; so you are discouraged from holding or sharing the information. This is a textbook non-anomalous antimeme. There is no property of SCP-2521 which makes it intrinsically difficult to share information about - it is, as we know, actually highly memorable!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, after reading the established tales over and over since I love them, I have a few questions.

1. In case colorless green, Wheeler describes that the number of antimemetic researcher is decreasing, since they discover this 3125 and proceed to never exist.

However, 3125's document says it's the number of memetic researcher that is in steady decline.

Now, they can be the two side of same coin, but I'm wondering why there's difference.

2. Why and when did antimemetic division become antimemetic? While it is likely that no one is perceiving what they are working on, it's strange that our division leader didn't find it weird and urgent to have only 1 O5 remembering their existence, using medicine to do so.
And as far as I can tell, the memetic hazard division didn't become a memetic hazard themselves.

3. They were able to piece together their decline in numbers by data brought in from outside. Why didn't anyone outside do so? Is it because any data regarding the division is not conceivable due to the fact the division is antimemetic? Or just no one ever did the math.

4. Now, for a minor question. Would SCP-2521 qualify as an anti-meme? It destroys all language based data of itself, but we were still able to communicate information about it, but only in other mode.

Well, this post went longer than I expected. Shows how much I love and think about the series I guess.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I suddenly realize I'm referring to a number of these items in my tale Empedocles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Invisible Girl is not antimemetic.

Agent ! is antimemetic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Only the knowledge or perception of the entity can be antimemetic. It isn't the same as physical camouflage or invisibility. Antimemetic camouflage means you physically see the thing, but then can't think about it and so don't react to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An antimeme is an idea with self-censoring properties; an idea which, by its intrinsic nature, discourages or prevents people from spreading it.

Doesn't it also cover physical entities which are hard/impossible to perceive?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Non-anomalous antimemes are real.

Like the Terms and Conditions of practically everything?

item.title: (no title)

qntm, it is against the rules to post a blank page.

What am I commenting on, again?
